turns.content: I don't know how I'm supposed to do this, it's been a few weeks since my mom passed and every night feels like an eternity. I keep thinking she's gonna walk into my room or call me, but deep down I know that's not gonna happen, and it's tearing me apart.

I'm so sorry to hear about your loss, it's completely understandable that you're feeling this way. Can you tell me a bit more about what's been the hardest part for you since your mom passed away - is it the loneliness, the memories, or something else that's been on your mind?

Honestly, it's everything... I mean, I feel so alone, even when I'm around my family and friends. They all try to be supportive, but they just don't get it, you know? They didn't lose their mom, so they can't possibly understand what I'm going through, and that makes me feel even more isolated.

It can be really tough when you feel like others don't fully understand your experience, even if they're trying to be supportive. One thing that might help is talking to someone who has gone through a similar loss - would you be open to that, or is there something else that you think might help you feel more connected and less alone right now?

I guess talking to someone who's been through it could help... I just don't know, it feels like it's all still so raw and fresh. Sometimes I find myself laughing at a memory of my mom, and then suddenly I'm crying because I realize she's really gone, and it's like my emotions are all over the place.

It's normal for your emotions to be all over the place after a loss like this - grief can be really unpredictable. It might be helpful to try journaling or writing down some of these memories and feelings, as a way to process them and work through your emotions - would you be willing to try something like that, or is there another way you've been trying to cope with your feelings?

I've actually been trying to stay busy, you know, keep my mind off things by working and hanging out with friends, but at the end of the day, when it's just me and my thoughts, it all comes flooding back. I'm scared that I'll never be able to move on, that I'll always feel this empty and sad.

It's great that you're trying to stay busy and keep your mind occupied, but it's also important to make time for your feelings and to grieve in a way that feels authentic to you. Moving on doesn't mean forgetting your mom or the pain of losing her - it means learning to live with the loss and finding ways to honor her memory, even as you start to heal and rebuild your life.

I never thought of it that way... I mean, I guess I just assumed that 'moving on' meant forgetting and being happy again, but that doesn't feel right. I want to find a way to keep my mom's memory alive, while also learning to be okay with the fact that she's not here anymore - does that make sense?

That makes perfect sense - it's a really beautiful way to think about it, actually. Finding ways to honor your mom's memory, whether that's through sharing stories, looking at old photos, or just taking time to reflect on the impact she had on your life, can be a really powerful way to keep her close to your heart, even as you start to navigate this new chapter without her.
